name: Secrets Baseline
description: Comprehensive secret and credential hygiene: blocks secrets from entering prompts and tool calls, redacts secrets in tool output, prevents reading well-known credential files, and catches hardcoded AWS access key patterns. Uses detection-based rules with fail-mode open to minimise false-positive disruption while still surfacing issues.

rules.name: Block secrets detected in user prompts
rules.description: Scans user prompts for API keys, tokens, passwords, and other credentials using the built-in gitleaks ruleset. Prevents credentials from being submitted to AI context.

rules.name: Block secrets detected in tool call inputs
rules.description: Scans the inputs to every tool call for credentials before they are executed. Prevents secrets from being written to files, run in commands, or sent to MCP servers.

rules.name: Redact secrets found in tool output
rules.description: Scans tool output for credentials after execution and redacts them before they reach the AI context. Does not block — allows execution to proceed with sanitised results.

rules.name: Block reading well-known credential files
rules.description: Prevents AI tools from reading .aws/credentials, SSH private keys, .env files, and /etc/shadow. These files contain secrets that should never be exposed to AI context.

rules.name: Block hardcoded AWS access key patterns in prompts
rules.description: Detects the AKIA/ASIA/AROA AWS key ID pattern in user prompts. These 20-character keys starting with AKIA are always credentials and should never appear in AI context.

rules.name: Warn on commands that dump environment variables
rules.description: printenv and bare env commands dump all environment variables, which may include secrets. Logs a warning without blocking.
